skill.name: ena-database
skill.description: Access European Nucleotide Archive records, metadata, sequence files, taxonomy data, and bulk download guidance for genomics workflows.
skill.summary: Retrieve and plan ENA sequence, read, assembly, taxonomy, and metadata access through documented public APIs.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, network, sensitive data, and weak cryptography patterns. Manual review found documentation for public ENA APIs, FTP download locations, ENA accession examples, and MD5 checksum lookup references, with no executable skill code, prompt injection, credential access, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Public Network API Examples
low_findings.description: The skill documents requests to public ENA REST endpoints. This is expected for the skill purpose, but users should understand that prompts may lead to outbound requests when an agent has network tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines show direct Python requests examples to official ebi.ac.uk ENA endpoints. The behavior is clearly documented and aligned with the skill purpose, with no evidence of credential exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Bulk FTP Download Guidance
low_findings.description: The reference explains how to obtain FASTQ and submitted files through ENA FTP locations. This is legitimate genomics functionality, but it can cause large data transfers if executed by an enabled tool.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The FTP references are official ENA data transfer guidance, not hidden network destinations. The remaining risk is operational, because bulk downloads can consume bandwidth and storage.

low_findings.title: Command-Line Tool Examples Are Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The static external command detections are mostly Markdown code spans and bash examples for enaBrowserTools. The skill contains instructions, not executable scripts that invoke shell commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown documentation and example commands. I found no wrapper script, subprocess call, or automatic command execution path in the scanned skill files.

low_findings.title: Sensitive and Cryptography Static Hits Are Naming Collisions
low_findings.description: SAM and SAMEA strings are ENA sample accession examples, not Windows SAM database access. MD5 appears as a checksum lookup and file integrity metadata field, not as password hashing or custom cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding ENA API tables define accession filters and CRAM reference checksum lookup. There is no evidence of local credential file access or weak cryptography used for security decisions.

content.value_statement: Researchers need reliable access to ENA records without memorizing many endpoints. This skill guides searches, metadata retrieval, sequence downloads, and bulk access planning.
actual_capabilities: Explains ENA object types, including studies, samples, runs, assemblies, sequences, analyses, and taxonomy records.
Shows how to query ENA Portal API endpoints for metadata searches and field discovery.
Guides Browser API retrieval for XML, text, FASTA, and cross-reference records by accession.
Documents file report fields for FASTQ, submitted files, SRA files, checksums, and file sizes.
Provides rate limit, retry, pagination, and bulk download guidance for larger datasets.
Covers taxonomy and CRAM reference lookup endpoints used in genomics workflows.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include executable helper scripts.
It depends on live ENA services, which may change availability or response formats.
It does not validate biological interpretations or downstream analysis quality.
Large file downloads still require user-managed storage, bandwidth, and compliance checks.

use_cases.title: Find Study Metadata
use_cases.description: Plan ENA Portal API queries that return samples, studies, runs, or assemblies matching accession and taxonomy filters.

use_cases.title: Prepare Sequencing Downloads
use_cases.description: Identify FASTQ, submitted, or SRA file locations and choose an API, FTP, or command-line download approach.

use_cases.title: Resolve Taxonomy and References
use_cases.description: Look up organism names, taxonomy identifiers, cross-references, and CRAM reference sequences for pipeline setup.

prompt_templates.title: Find an Accession
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ENA Database skill to explain how I can retrieve metadata for accession [ACCESSION]. Include the best endpoint and output format.

prompt_templates.title: Search Samples by Study
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ENA Database skill to design a Portal API search for all samples in study [STUDY_ACCESSION]. Include useful fields and pagination advice.

prompt_templates.title: Plan FASTQ Downloads
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ENA Database skill to plan FASTQ retrieval for these run accessions: [RUN_ACCESSIONS]. Compare file report, FTP, and enaBrowserTools options.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Robust Pipeline Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ENA Database skill to design a rate-limited workflow that finds assemblies for [ORGANISM], retrieves metadata, validates file checksums, and records citations.

output_examples.output: Use the Browser API for direct accession metadata. The response can be requested as XML, then parsed for run, experiment, sample, and study links.

output_examples.input: Find FASTQ files for a sequencing run.
output_examples.output: Use the file report endpoint with read run fields. The result should identify FASTQ locations, file sizes, and checksum values for validation.

output_examples.input: Search E. coli assemblies with metadata filters.
output_examples.output: Use the Portal API assembly result type with a taxonomy tree filter. Limit returned fields to assembly accessions, scientific names, dates, and assembly quality fields.

best_practices: Use accession-specific endpoints for direct lookups and Portal API searches for broad metadata queries.
Respect ENA rate limits and add retry logic for 429 and transient server responses.
Record study, sample, run, and assembly accessions so downloaded data can be cited and reproduced.
anti_patterns: Do not loop over thousands of API calls when file reports, pagination, or bulk downloads fit better.
Do not parse XML with regular expressions when standard XML parsers are available.
Do not download large sequencing files without checking storage, checksum, and usage requirements first.

faq.question: Does this skill download ENA data by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and query guidance. Downloads require an enabled tool or user action.

faq.question: Which ENA APIs are covered?
faq.answer: It covers Portal API, Browser API, Taxonomy REST API, Cross-Reference Service, and CRAM Reference Registry endpoints.

faq.question: Can it help with FASTQ files?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains file report fields and bulk download options for FASTQ and related sequencing files.

faq.question: Is it useful for taxonomy lookups?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents taxon ID, scientific name, and suggestion endpoints for organism metadata.

faq.question: Does it include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The files are Markdown documentation with examples and API references.

faq.question: What should users check before bulk downloads?
faq.answer: Users should confirm file size, storage capacity, checksum validation, rate limits, and data usage requirements.
